summary: MCP conformance tests based on the official MCP specification. The official @modelcontextprotocol/conformance suite only supports HTTP/stdio transports and cannot directly test QUIC-based transports. 10 spec-based conformance tests verify the AAFP MCP transport binding meets MCP spec requirements: transport connect, initialize handshake, tools/list, tools/call, resources/list+read, JSON-RPC ping, error handling, graceful close, sequential operations, and large result transmission. All 10 tests pass.

details.notes: Official @modelcontextprotocol/conformance suite exists (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/conformance, v0.1.11). Run via npx or GitHub Action. Supports server mode (HTTP URL) and client mode (subprocess command). Only supports HTTP/stdio transports — cannot plug in QUIC. Falls back to spec-based conformance tests.
Transport establishes QUIC connection with AAFP handshake. Client service is not closed after connect.
MCP initialize handshake completes. Server info (name=aafp-conformance-server, version=1.0.0) available via peer_info().
tools/list returns echo tool with name, description, and input schema.
tools/call executes echo tool with message argument, returns 'Echo: conformance test' text content.
resources/list returns Hello Resource (test://conformance/hello). resources/read returns 'Hello, conformance world!' text content.
JSON-RPC level ping (not AAFP PING) sent via peer.send_request(PingRequest). Response received successfully.
Calling non-existent tool returns METHOD_NOT_FOUND error.
client_service.cancel() succeeds. Server terminates within 10s. No panic or hang.
6 sequential operations (list tools, call tool, list resources, read resource, call tool again, ping) all succeed on same connection.
50KB message echoed back correctly. Result content length matches expected (50007 bytes = 'Echo: ' + 50000).

metrics.official_suite_reason: Only supports HTTP/stdio, not QUIC. Spec-based tests created instead.
